steps.title: Identify the true subject
steps.content: The blank's subject is "lost works" — a plural noun. The long dash phrase "—no copy of which exists—" is a parenthetical interruption and does not change the subject. Strip it out: "lost works ______ known to antiquarians."

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
A plural subject requires a plural verb form.
  • Choice A — "have become": plural present perfect. Agrees with the plural subject "lost works." Correct.
  • Choice B — "is becoming": singular present progressive. "Is" is singular and does not agree with "lost works." Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "has become": singular present perfect. "Has" is singular — same mismatch. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "becomes": singular simple present. Third-person singular "becomes" conflicts with the plural subject. Eliminated.
